summary.delta_vs_iteration_1: -0.044 (-4.4pp, but iter-3 has 5 more assertions and stricter riff counting)

evals.name: consumer-hot-sauce
failures: B2B compound patterns leaked (Brineworks, Scorchcraft) despite skip claim
Gallery H/W/C not shown (non-interactive session limitation)
iter2_failures_fixed: Technique toolkit now offered (new assertion PASS)

evals.name: consumer-streetwear
failures: Zero conflict flags (fashion relaxation went too far — no flags at all)
iter2_failures_fixed: Desired-Self Naming: PASS (was FAIL — now applied with visible output)
Trend-Cycle Audit: PASS (was FAIL — now applied)
Memetic/Viral Engineering: PASS (was FAIL — now applied)

evals.name: consumer-mens-skincare
failures: Riff volume: 2 of 8 directions at 8-9 new unique riffs (stricter counting than iter-2)
iter2_failures_fixed: Technique toolkit now offered (new assertion PASS)

evals.name: enterprise-cybersecurity
failures: Integration/ecosystem stack test not visibly demonstrated (claimed but no output)
iter2_failures_fixed: Analyst/Press Headline: PASS (was FAIL — now tests both Gartner AND Forbes)

evals.name: enterprise-data-integration
failures: Stack test not explicitly run (integration grammar tested instead)
Tech Branding Patterns not explicitly named as technique
Systematic Affix Exploration incidental not deliberate
B2B riffing checklist: tech branding item not labeled correctly
iter2_failures_fixed: Prefix x Suffix Matrix: PASS (was FAIL)

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Technique toolkit dramatically improved technique coverage
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Legaltech went from 52% to 100%. Streetwear went from 85% to 95%. Data integration went from 76% to 82%. The toolkit's [A] all option ensures techniques that were previously forgotten now get applied.

cross_cutting_observations.finding: All iter-2 failures fixed except data integration stack test and technique labeling issues
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Of the 21 iter-2 failures, 17 are now fixed. The remaining 4 in data integration are technique-naming/labeling specificity issues rather than missing work.

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Stricter riff counting reveals marginal shortfalls
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Skincare: 2 of 8 directions at 8-9 new unique riffs. This was invisible in iter-2 because duplicates were counted. The stricter standard is correct but creates new borderline failures.

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Gallery H/W/C remains unfixable in non-interactive sessions
cross_cutting_observations.detail: The Hot/Warm/Cold gallery reaction format requires user interaction that subagents can't simulate. Consider removing this assertion from automated evals.

Hot sauce: Brineworks and Scorchcraft leaked through despite the skip directive. The compound skip guidance needs to be stronger, or the assertion needs to tolerate minor leakage from toolkit-driven techniques like Prefix × Suffix Matrix.

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Fashion conflict flagging went to zero
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Streetwear session produced zero conflict flags. The relaxed fashion guidance was interpreted as 'don't flag anything' rather than 'flag same-industry conflicts, ignore cross-industry ones.'

cross_cutting_observations.finding: Candidate volume increased significantly with toolkit
cross_cutting_observations.detail: Average candidates: 243 (iter-3) vs 184 (iter-2) — 32% increase. The toolkit's [A] all drives more generation passes, which produces more names. This is good for coverage but increases token usage (avg 148k vs 133k).
